title: E2E test: DM to offline recipient delivered on reconnect
description:
What: Integration test verifying the complete DM flow: Alice sends a Whisper to Bob who is offline; Bob reconnects and receives the queued DM.

Algorithm:
1. In relay/tests/ (alongside existing e2e tests), add a new test function test_dm_offline_delivery:
   a. Spawn a test relay (same pattern as existing integration tests)
   b. Connect Alice, authenticate via challenge-response
   c. Connect Bob, authenticate via challenge-response, then disconnect Bob's WebSocket
   d. Alice sends a Whisper request: {jsonrpc:'2.0', id:1, method:'whisper', params:{to: bob_pub_id, payload: <base64 of ClearMessage::Chat{text:'hello from alice'}>}}
   e. Alice receives OkResult {ok: true}
   f. Reconnect Bob, authenticate again
   g. Immediately after Bob receives AuthOk/DirectoryList, assert Bob receives a whisper_deliver notification:
      - method == 'whisper_deliver'
      - params.from == alice_pub_id
      - params.payload deserialized as ClearMessage::Chat {text: 'hello from alice'}

2. Also test: Alice whispers to ONLINE Bob → Bob receives it immediately (no queue involved)
   a. Both Alice and Bob connected
   b. Alice sends whisper
   c. Bob receives whisper_deliver immediately (without reconnect)

Oracle for test_dm_offline_delivery:
- Expected payload: serde_json::to_vec(&ClearMessage::Chat{text:'hello from alice'}).unwrap() — computed independently at test construction time, not from the code path under test
- Expected from: alice_pub_id (the hex-SHA256 of Alice's Ed25519 key)

Defensive rules:
- Tests must use real relay + real SQLite (no mocks) per CLAUDE.md test oracle discipline
- Use tokio::time::timeout on Bob's receive to fail fast rather than hang
- Independent oracle: construct expected payload bytes WITHOUT calling the /dm code path
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-relay test_dm_offline_delivery -- --nocapture passes; cargo test -p nie-relay test_dm_live_delivery passes; cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
